---
title: "Using AI Assistants with Local VSCode on SURF Research Cloud"
---

# Overview

This guide explains how to use AI assistants via your local VSCode while working on SURF Research Cloud. AI coding assistants can help with code completion, generation, debugging, and documentation.


::: {.callout-warning}
## Disclaimer

Utrecht University does not endorse using AI coding assistants. Users are solely responsible for:

- **Compliance:** Ensuring use complies with institutional policies on AI tools
- **Privacy:** Understanding that code may be sent to external AI services
- **Validation:** Reviewing and validating all AI-generated code
- **Licensing:** Checking terms of service and licensing requirements

Review [Utrecht University's AI policy and guidelines](https://www.uu.nl/en/organisation/ai-policy) before using AI coding assistants.
:::

## How It Works

When you connect your local VS Code to a Research Cloud workspace via SSH:

- **Your local VS Code** acts as the interface with AI assistant extensions installed locally
- **Code executes on the workspace** using the workspace's Python environments, libraries, and resources
- **AI assistants run locally** in your VS Code and can suggest code that runs remotely on the workspace

## Available AI Coding Assistants

Several AI coding assistants are available as VS Code extensions such as [GitHub Copilot](https://github.com/features/copilot), [Claude for VS Code](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/vs-code), [Qodo](https://www.codium.ai/qodo/), [Cursor](https://cursor.sh/). There are additional assistants available as well as extensions in VSCode.

Each assistant has different features, pricing, and privacy policies. Choose based on your needs and institutional guidelines.


## Prerequisites

1. [VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/) connected to your workspace via SSH. Follow our [Connecting to Research Cloud from VS Code](../manuals/vscode-to-researchcloud.qmd) guide first.
2. **Active subscription or account** for your chosen AI assistant
3. **API key or authentication** if required by the assistant

::: {.callout-tip}
**Recommended workspaces:** While AI assistants work with any workspace, those with Python environment managers pre-installed (Python Workbench, VRE Lab) make it easier to manage project dependencies.
:::


## Setup Instructions

Although the exact steps for each extension may vary, the setup process is generally similar for most AI assistants. You can also use this method for other IDEs that support SSH connections and extensions.

### Step 1: Connect to Your Workspace

1. Open VS Code on your local computer
2. Connect to your workspace via [Remote-SSH](../manuals/vscode-to-researchcloud.qmd)
3. Verify you're connected by checking for **"SSH: myworkspace"** in the bottom left corner

### Step 2: Install AI Assistant Extension

1. In VS Code, click the **Extensions** icon (left sidebar) or press `Cmd+Shift+X` (Mac) / `Ctrl+Shift+X` (Windows)
2. Search for your chosen AI assistant (e.g., GitHub Copilot, Claude, Cursor)
3. Click **Install**. Avoid installing extensions from third-party sources or unauthenticated publishers.
4. Follow the extension's authentication process (sign in, enter API key, etc.)
5. Verify the extension is active and ready to use
6. You can begin using the AI assistant extensions in your VS Code editor.


### Working with Workspace Environments

Since your code runs on the workspace:

1. **Select the correct Python interpreter:**
   - Press `Cmd+Shift+P` / `Ctrl+Shift+P`
   - Type "Python: Select Interpreter"
   - Choose from workspace environments (uv, conda, pyenv)

2. **Import workspace libraries:**
   - AI assistants can suggest code using libraries installed on the workspace
   - Make sure the environment has the necessary packages installed

3. **Run code on the workspace:**
   - Terminal commands execute on the workspace
   - File paths reference workspace directories

### Best Practices

- **Review all AI-generated code** before using it.  
- **Test thoroughly**: AI suggestions aren't always correct and may contain errors or security issues.
- **Be aware of data sensitivity**: avoid sending confidential code/data to AI services
- **Check licensing**: ensure AI-generated code doesn't violate licenses
- **Use AI as a tool, not a replacement** for understanding your code

## Privacy and Security Considerations

::: {.callout-important}
## Important Privacy Notes

- **Code is sent to external servers:** AI assistants send your code to their servers for processing
- **Avoid sensitive data:** Don't include passwords, API keys, or confidential information in code when using AI assistants
- **Check institutional policies:** Ensure your use complies with your institution's data protection requirements
- **Review terms of service:** Understand how your code and data are used by the AI service

Check each assistant's privacy policy and data handling practices before use.
:::

## Troubleshooting

**Extension not working after connecting to workspace**

- Some extensions may need to be installed on the workspace
- VS Code will prompt `Install in SSH: myworkspace` - click to install
- Any new extensions installed in such a manner will be installed on the workspace and not your local VSCode

**Authentication issues**

- Re-authenticate with the extension service
- Check API key validity

If you have any issues or questions, you can [contact us here.](https://www.uu.nl/en/research/research-data-management/contact)
